agent_notes: Southwark is unusually strong for PAINTS: Bermondsey/Borough supply markets, wine bars and galleries, while Peckham/Camberwell supply bookish and neighbourhood texture.
Creative Walks are limited to PAINTS-approved routes only: Southwark Park, Peckham Rye Park & Common, South Bank to Tate Modern/Bankside, and Rotherhithe to Greenwich via Thames Path.
Google ratings/review counts and exact group walk-in tolerance for 8-12 must still be host-confirmed before publishing; many venues publish hours but not group walk-in rules.
White Cube Bermondsey appears temporarily between Bermondsey exhibitions on 2026-07-04, so it is included as a cultural backup rather than a current-exhibition anchor.
Southwark's best drinks venues are genuinely strong, but several fail the strict same-category 10-minute backup rule; they are demoted to Tier B even where the taste fit is higher.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says dinner Wednesday-Saturday and lunch Thursday-Saturday; Instagram lists lunch Thu-Sat 12:00-15:00 and dinner Wed-Sat 17:30-21:30. Bank holiday/private hire handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site and Instagram say no reservations; 8-12 person walk-in is possible only early and UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best as an early Saturday lunch or Wednesday dinner arrival; communal/counter seating makes 12 fragile.
venues.capacity_notes: Small railway-arch wine bar in the Gergovie Wines warehouse; queue risk at peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: A benchmark Southwark natural-wine room with warehouse texture, producer-led bottles and food that gives strangers something precise to talk about. It is famous enough to take a penalty, but still feels materially tied to Maltby Street rather than polished for tourists. It would be Tier A on taste, but the strict 10-minute drinks-backup rule demotes it.
venues.anecdote_story: The restaurant sits inside the Gergovie Wines warehouse, connecting the room directly to a specialist natural-wine importer.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural and low-intervention wines, seasonal British/European small plates, wine-shop off-sales.
venues.safety_notes: Maltby Street and Bermondsey Street are active early evening; use London Bridge or Bermondsey routes after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 20 min walk - no qualifying drinks backup within 10 min; demotion avoided only because Maltby Street has multiple informal adjacent bars, but host should treat this as an operational caveat.
venues.risks_watchouts: Fails strict 10-min same-category backup rule unless another Maltby Street wine-bar backup is host-confirmed; peak queues are real.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Tue-Wed 17:00-23:00, Thu-Fri 12:00-23:00, Sat 11:00-23:00, Sun 11:00-22:00. Bank holiday/private hire handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official and recent Instagram copy say book or simply walk in; 8-12 person walk-ins are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable early weekday or Sunday afternoon; call ahead for 12.
venues.capacity_notes: Neighbourhood wine bar and shop with small plates; easier than 40 Maltby Street for a seated group.
venues.why_fits_paints: The venue is built around questioning wine assumptions, which is exactly the right Show & Tell mechanism. It is independent, story-led, dog-friendly and neighbourly rather than glossy. The unusual-regions list gives a host an easy conversational spine, but the backup radius keeps it in Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Eater reported founder Sunny Hodge opened it to challenge what London drinkers think a wine bar should be, with rare terroirs and origin stories at the centre.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Undiscovered wines from lesser-known regions, small plates, wine-shop bottles.
venues.safety_notes: Rodney Road is residential but close to Elephant & Castle; use well-lit main-road route via New Kent Road or Walworth Road after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 14 min walk - no qualifying same-category backup within strict 10 min; closest stronger alternative is Little Cellars.
venues.risks_watchouts: Strict backup radius is the main caveat; group walk-in must be confirmed.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Tue-Fri 17:30-23:00 and Sat 13:00-23:00; Star Wine List says Sat 12:00-23:00. Bank holiday/private hire handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Reservations are promoted; Instagram/review signal suggests some walk-ins, but 8-12 person walk-ins are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Use early Saturday or Tuesday; group of 12 likely needs a heads-up call.
venues.capacity_notes: Neighbourhood wine bar and bistro from three local founders, with a large bottle selection.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has the sweet spot of serious wine without preciousness, and Queens Road gives it a local counterweight away from London Bridge. The founders' neighbourhood story is warm enough for matched strangers. It is stronger than a generic bar, but the strict 10-minute drinks-backup rule demotes it to Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site says it was founded in 2019 by three old friends and long-time South East London residents: Luke, Ben and Helen.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Eclectic wines, snacks and small plates; bottle shop option.
venues.safety_notes: Queens Road Peckham station is close and busy; use main-road routing after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 16 min walk or short bus - no strict 10-min same-category backup found; closest high-fit alternative is Bar Levan.
venues.risks_watchouts: Strict 10-min backup rule not met; group walk-in confirmation needed.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Wed-Fri 17:30-23:00 and Sat 17:00-23:00; SquareMeal lists Wed-Sat 17:00-23:00. Bank holiday/private hire handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site promotes WhatsApp reservations; old Instagram says space for bookings and walk-ins, but 8-12 person walk-ins are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better for 8 than 12; early Wednesday/Thursday only.
venues.capacity_notes: Small Camberwell sibling to Peckham Cellars; operationally fragile for large walk-ins.
venues.why_fits_paints: It extends the Peckham Cellars sensibility into a quieter Camberwell room, which helps Southwark avoid a Borough-only list. The wine and small-plates format fits Show & Tell, but the published booking emphasis caps it at Tier B. It is a useful backup if PAINTS can pre-call.
venues.anecdote_story: Harpers reported Little Cellars was opened by the people behind Peckham Cellars, a Michelin Bib Gourmand restaurant and bar.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine, snacks and small plates.
venues.safety_notes: Camberwell Church Street is active; route attendees to bus stops or Denmark Hill via main roads.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 14 min walk - no qualifying strict 10-min drinks backup found.
venues.risks_watchouts: Reservation emphasis and small room make it Tier B.

venues.opening_hours: Official site confirms location and wine-bar/kitchen/shop format but live hours are not clearly surfaced in search extract; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site promotes booking a table for lunch or dinner; 8-12 person walk-ins are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely only with early arrival or pre-call; cap at Tier B until walk-in acceptance is confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Bistro/wine bar near Peckham Rye station; popular and potentially tight.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday late afternoon, before dinner service.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Left Bank-coded Peckham wine room with strong natural-wine signal and enough visual warmth for a group. It is stylish without losing neighbourhood specificity. Operational certainty is weaker than the score would like, so it stays Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site positions it as a bar, kitchen and wine shop just off Rye Lane, under the Levan identity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural wine, French-leaning food, wine-shop bottles.
venues.safety_notes: Very close to Peckham Rye station; Rye Lane is high-footfall but busy late.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 16 min walk or short bus - no strict 10-min same-category backup found.
venues.risks_watchouts: Published walk-in and group policy not clear; backup radius not strict.

venues.opening_hours: Official visit page lists Tue-Fri 10:00-17:00, Sat 09:00-17:00, Sun 10:00-16:00, Monday closed; festive/bank holiday changes published separately.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; individual trader seating varies.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent as a hosted roam if split into pairs for stalls and reconvene at fixed points.
venues.capacity_notes: High-capacity market but narrow/crowded lanes; avoid Saturday midday.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is the obvious Southwark market, but the Victorian fabric, specialist producers and deep food history still justify inclusion. PAINTS should curate specific stalls rather than let the group drift. The market is resilient, central and easy to back up.
venues.anecdote_story: London Museum describes Borough as one of London's oldest food-trading hubs, now housed in distinctive Victorian buildings.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Produce, cheese, bakery, coffee, street food, specialist food traders.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall and station-adjacent; pick a clear meeting point to avoid crowd separation.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min walk or short bus; within market backup allowance.
venues.risks_watchouts: Tourist crowding; top-3 obviousness penalty applied.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Fri 17:30-21:00, Sat 10:00-17:00, Sun 11:00-16:00; Southwark Council lists Sat 09:00-16:00 and Sun 11:00-16:00, closed Mon-Fri. Host must confirm Friday evening against current trader opening.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; trader seating and queues vary.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for a moving 8-12 group if started early, with a fixed end point.
venues.capacity_notes: Narrow Ropewalk railway-arch market; atmospheric but congested at peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Ropewalk has exactly the slightly hidden, sensory market quality PAINTS wants: arches, smoke, cheese, wine and maker stalls. It is less polished than Borough and better for conversation if timed early. The nearby Bermondsey galleries and wine bars make it easy to extend.
venues.anecdote_story: Southwark Council describes it as a small bustling market with fresh produce including bread, cakes, meats and cheeses.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, produce, bakery, cheese, drinks and arch-based traders.
venues.safety_notes: Safe by day/early evening with high footfall; keep group out of narrow roadway pinch points.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min walk or short bus; within market backup allowance.
venues.risks_watchouts: Hours conflict between official market site and council page for Friday; host must confirm.

venues.opening_hours: Official Blue Market site lists Wed-Sat 10:00-17:00; Southwark Council lists Mon-Sat 09:00-17:00. Host must confirm current trader days.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access community market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable as a community-history market walk, not a destination food crawl.
venues.capacity_notes: Open market square with regular traders and Saturday community layer.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is not the prettiest Southwark market, but it gives a stronger local counterweight than Borough. The Blue has real Bermondsey memory and a community-market feel. It is Tier B because the trader mix is less consistently maker-led.
venues.anecdote_story: The Blue name is tied to Bermondsey's historic Blue Anchor area and market-place identity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh produce, household goods, local traders, community stalls and Saturday events.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; use Bermondsey or South Bermondsey station routes and avoid dispersal late.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 25 min walk or short bus - within relaxed market transit backup allowance.
venues.risks_watchouts: Less curated and less reliably food/craft-led than PAINTS Tier A market picks.

venues.opening_hours: Copeland Park event page lists 11:00-18:00 for a Salvage Yard event; 2026 Instagram/Eventbrite posts list specific 2026 dates and 10:00-17:00. Event date recurrence is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access/ticketed flea event depending on edition; group entry should be straightforward if event is running.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Very good for browsing in pairs with regroup points.
venues.capacity_notes: Indoors/outdoors flea and vintage market with 50-70+ traders when active.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the most PAINTS-coded Southwark market when it is actually on: rummage, furniture, odd objects, clothes and stories built into every stall. Copeland Park adds a cultural anchor and food/drink backup. It is included with a recurrence caveat.
venues.anecdote_story: Copeland Park describes it as a yard of salvaged old things with hand-picked traders bringing furniture, curiosities, industrial fixtures and unexpected objects.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Vintage furniture, salvage, clothing, curiosities, collectibles, food/drink nearby.
venues.safety_notes: Copeland Park and Rye Lane are busy; use daytime only for first PAINTS runs.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 25 min by bus/walk - within relaxed market transit backup allowance.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not weekly; must confirm active event date and ticketing.

venues.opening_hours: Official page lists Mon-Wed 12:00-23:00, Thu 12:00-00:00, Fri 12:00-01:00, Sat 11:00-01:00, Sun 11:00-22:00 and says open bank holidays.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access food/community market; table seating varies by trader and time.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally easy, but less intimate; pick off-peak and a defined table zone.
venues.capacity_notes: Large covered/open food market; redevelopment/closure risk in 2026.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is not as aesthetically subtle as Maltby or Borough, but it is robust, weather-resilient and easy for a mixed group. The sustainable community-market framing gives it more value than a standard food hall. Tier B because it risks feeling generic and may be leaving the site.
venues.anecdote_story: Mercato describes it as London's first sustainable community food market; 2026 press reports say the Elephant site is expected to operate through spring/summer 2026 before redevelopment.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Multiple independent food traders, bars, covered seating and outdoor piazza.
venues.safety_notes: Use Elephant & Castle station routes; avoid very late Friday/Saturday dispersal.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 20 min walk or short bus - within relaxed market transit backup allowance.
venues.risks_watchouts: Redevelopment/closure timing in 2026 is the biggest operational risk.

venues.venue_name: Southwark Park Art, Lake and Ada Salter Loop
venues.opening_hours: Open public park; Southwark Park Galleries within the park list Wed-Sun 12:00-17:00. Park-specific daily gate hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route; no booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 in daylight with a clear start/end.
venues.capacity_notes: Suggested stops: Southwark Park Galleries, lake, Ada Salter Garden, 1884 bandstand.
venues.why_fits_paints: Southwark Park is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist and offers art, reform politics, water and civic-park design in one compact loop. It is calmer than Bankside and still visually rich. The gallery gives a weather-aware cultural layer.
venues.anecdote_story: The park opened in 1869; its bandstand dates from 1884, and the Ada Salter Garden anchors a local social-reform story.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Public park landscape, lake, garden, bandstand, Southwark Park Galleries when open.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Safety-First park; daylight only for launch events and avoid isolated corners at dusk.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within the park / approx. 2 min from suggested start; weather backup within 5 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Park gate hours and gallery exhibition dates must be checked before event day.

venues.venue_name: Peckham Rye Park & Common: Blake, Sexby Garden and River Peck Loop
venues.opening_hours: Open public park/common; exact gate hours for park gardens UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route; no booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for daylight walk with 3-4 fixed stops.
venues.capacity_notes: Suggested stops: Common edge, Sexby Garden, Japanese garden, River Peck remnant / lake.
venues.why_fits_paints: Peckham Rye is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist and has enough literary, garden and urban-nature story to sustain a proper hosted walk. It pairs well with Rye Books or Lala Books as indoor fallback. It is stronger by day than evening.
venues.anecdote_story: William Blake is said to have had an early vision of a tree filled with angels on Peckham Rye; the park formally opened in 1894 and includes the Sexby Garden.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Common, ornamental gardens, Japanese garden, lake, woodland and literary history.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Safety-First park; daylight only and finish before dusk.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min from northern/common-side start; weather backup within 5 min if route starts near North Cross Road.
venues.risks_watchouts: If starting deeper in the park, the backup exceeds 5 min; use North Cross Road as the formal meeting point.

venues.venue_name: Bankside Riverside: South Bank to Tate Modern
venues.opening_hours: Open public riverside route; Tate Modern lists Sun-Thu 10:00-18:00 and Fri-Sat 10:00-21:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access riverside route; Tate Modern free collection is walk-in, paid exhibitions may be timed-entry.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent in daylight/early evening with clear regroup points.
venues.capacity_notes: Suggested stops: Blackfriars view, Shakespeare's Globe exterior, Millennium Bridge, Tate Turbine Hall/Bankside.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of the explicitly permitted non-park creative routes and it puts art, theatre, river engineering and city views into a short social walk. It is obvious but operationally robust. The host can end at Tate or Bankside Gallery if weather turns.
venues.anecdote_story: The route sits on the Thames Path and passes Tate Modern, Shakespeare's Globe and the Millennium Bridge, making a compact story of Bankside's cultural reinvention.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Riverside public realm, Tate Modern, Globe exterior, Millennium Bridge, Thames views.
venues.safety_notes: Permitted riverside route; high-footfall and transit-rich, but avoid late-night dispersal under bridges.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min from route end and can be made within 5 min from formal start if starting at Bankside Pier/Tate.
venues.risks_watchouts: Can feel tourist-heavy; choose a tighter Bankside start rather than the full South Bank crowd zone.

venues.opening_hours: Open public Thames Path route; exact closures/diversions UNVERIFIED - host must confirm on event day.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access riverside route; no booking required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good in daylight for a fitter group; too long for a gentle 2-hour first PAINTS walk unless shortened.
venues.capacity_notes: Suggested Southwark-side stops: Brunel Museum exterior, Mayflower pub exterior, Rotherhithe village, Greenland Pier / river views.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is an explicitly permitted PAINTS non-park route, and Rotherhithe has dock, tunnel and river texture that rewards attention. It is Tier B because the full route to Greenwich is long and operationally more exposed. A shortened Rotherhithe loop is safer for launch.
venues.anecdote_story: The Brunel Museum marks the Thames Tunnel story, while the Thames Path official route follows the river through this docklands stretch.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Riverside path, Brunel/dock history, old pubs, piers and Thames views.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; avoid isolated riverside stretches after dark and check diversions.
venues.backup_venue: The Brunel Museum / nearby cafe area
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 0-5 min from Rotherhithe start; weather backup details UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.risks_watchouts: Length and weather exposure; use a shortened version unless attendees expect a long walk.

venues.venue_name: Tate Modern - Ana Mendieta / Frida Kahlo / Tracey Emin season
venues.opening_hours: Official visit page lists Sun-Thu 10:00-18:00 and Fri-Sat 10:00-21:00; paid exhibition ticket availability for PAINTS date UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free collection is walk-in; major paid exhibitions are timed-entry and must be checked for group availability.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent if timed-entry booked or collection route used; too crowded at peak Saturday afternoon.
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum; use one exhibition plus a fixed coffee meeting point.
venues.why_fits_paints: The July 2026 programme has unusually strong female-artist anchors, with Ana Mendieta opening 15 July and Frida/Tracey Emin already live according to Tate/press signals. The building itself is an easy shared object. It is obvious, but the exhibition quality more than clears the bar.
venues.anecdote_story: The Guardian's July 2026 Mendieta preview frames the retrospective around her body, land, exile and contested legacy; Tate also lists Frida and Tracey Emin season activity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Modern and contemporary art; July 2026 paid exhibitions around Ana Mendieta, Frida Kahlo and Tracey Emin plus free collection.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall Bankside location; direct routes to Blackfriars, London Bridge or Southwark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 2 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Timed-entry availability and exhibition opening dates matter; Ana Mendieta starts 2026-07-15, after the run date.

venues.venue_name: South London Gallery - Paulo Nimer Pjota: Encantados
venues.opening_hours: Official exhibition page lists Main Gallery Wed 12:00-21:00 and Thu-Sun 12:00-18:00 for Paulo Nimer Pjota: Encantados, 1 May-23 Aug 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free exhibition; walk-in presumed, but group handling for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for a hosted gallery day with a short cafe start.
venues.capacity_notes: Main gallery plus nearby Fire Station space/cafe; enough cultural density for 2 hours.
venues.why_fits_paints: The exhibition itself justifies the event: large-scale paintings mixing art history, mythology, folk tales, graffiti and Brazilian music sampling logic. The SLG also anchors Peckham/Camberwell rather than Bankside. It is a PAINTS-perfect gallery pick.
venues.anecdote_story: SLG says Pjota's painting borrows from Brazilian hip-hop/rap sampling and from his teenage experience as a graffiti artist.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Large-scale oil, tempera and acrylic paintings.
venues.safety_notes: Peckham Road is busy and bus-served; use main-road routing to Denmark Hill or Peckham Rye.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk; cultural/bookish backup rather than same-category gallery.
venues.risks_watchouts: Strict same-category backup within 10 min is weak; closest gallery backup may be unavailable.

venues.venue_name: Fashion and Textile Museum - Paint! Pattern! Print!
venues.opening_hours: Official page lists Paint! Pattern! Print! 27 Mar-13 Sep 2026, Tue-Sat 11:00-18:00, last entry 17:15; group/ticket availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed museum; walk-in/timed-entry availability for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if tickets are available; maximum 15 noted for selected highlight tours.
venues.capacity_notes: Compact museum; use a timed visit and debrief at Bermondsey Street cafe.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday or Thursday 15:00 highlight tour where available.
venues.why_fits_paints: Textiles, pattern and process are strong PAINTS conversation objects. The current Collier/Campbell exhibition is hands-on in spirit without requiring everyone to be fashion-literate. Bermondsey Street provides excellent backups.
venues.anecdote_story: The exhibition traces Susan Collier and Sarah Campbell from painted beginnings to bold textiles, fashion and homeware designs.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Textiles, fashion, pattern design, related workshops and highlight tours.
venues.safety_notes: Bermondsey Street is high-footfall and well connected to London Bridge.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Ticketing and Tuesday-Saturday opening only; verify current exhibition availability.

venues.venue_name: Bankside Gallery - Quentin Blake: Good Companions
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Quentin Blake: Good Companions 1-26 July 2026 and 11:00-18:00 daily; free gallery.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free exhibition; walk-in suitable, but group handling for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good as a gentle gallery day or Tate backup.
venues.capacity_notes: Compact contemporary gallery dedicated to water-based media and printmaking.
venues.why_fits_paints: Quentin Blake's companionship theme is almost too on-the-nose for PAINTS, but it works: sociable, legible, and discussion-friendly. The gallery's printmaking/watercolour identity makes it more intimate than Tate. It is also operationally easy.
venues.anecdote_story: Bankside says the Good Companions works explore relationships between people and animals or birds, with new work made since 2019.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Quentin Blake drawings/works, contemporary watercolour and original printmaking.
venues.safety_notes: Bankside riverfront is busy and well lit; easy Blackfriars/London Bridge exits.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 2 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small space; avoid peak Tate overflow times.

venues.venue_name: White Cube Bermondsey - Georg Baselitz: Back Again
venues.opening_hours: Official location page lists Tue-Sat 10:00-18:00, Sun 12:00-18:00, Monday closed. Ocula lists Georg Baselitz: Back Again 10 Jun-30 Aug 2026; official White Cube page should be checked for live Bermondsey status.
venues.walk_in_policy: No admission charge; walk-in likely, but group handling for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent physical capacity, but check whether Bermondsey galleries are between installs.
venues.capacity_notes: Very large commercial gallery with courtyard and multiple exhibition spaces.
venues.why_fits_paints: White Cube's scale and Bermondsey Street setting are strong, and Baselitz gives a major-painting hook. It is Tier B because official live search results were clearer for location than for the exact Bermondsey exhibition status on 2026-07-04. Use as a backup unless host confirms.
venues.anecdote_story: White Cube says the Bermondsey gallery opened in 2011 in a 1970s building redesigned by Casper Mueller Kneer and is one of Europe's largest commercial gallery spaces.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary art; likely Baselitz paintings in summer 2026 subject to confirmation.
venues.safety_notes: Bermondsey Street is active, safe and close to London Bridge.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current exhibition confirmation is the caveat; commercial-gallery install periods can close rooms.

venues.venue_name: Southwark Park Galleries - 2026 Spring Programme / Annual Open
venues.opening_hours: Visit page lists Lake Gallery and Dilston Gallery Wed-Sun 12:00-17:00. Spring exhibitions close 5 July 2026; Annual Open starts 16 July 2026 per Instagram/council signals.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free gallery; group handling for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good as part of a park/gallery hybrid, weaker as standalone between exhibition dates.
venues.capacity_notes: Two park galleries; exhibitions are date-sensitive.
venues.why_fits_paints: The park-gallery pairing is specific and gently off-grid, which PAINTS likes. It is Tier B because the run date sits right at a programme turnover. Stronger when paired with the Southwark Park creative walk.
venues.anecdote_story: Southwark Park Galleries runs Lake and Dilston gallery spaces inside one of London's early municipal parks.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary art exhibitions; 2026 programme includes Saelia Aparicio/Emily Waszak and Annual Open timing signals.
venues.safety_notes: Use daylight and PAINTS-approved Southwark Park route; avoid dusk.
venues.backup_venue: The Blue Market / Southwark Park walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10-12 min to The Blue; within park for walk backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Exhibition changeover around early July; host must confirm what is actually open.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says cafe bookshop open 7 days; Instagram says 10:00-17:00 seven days and Sunday 10:00-16:00. Host must confirm current July 2026 hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop/cafe appears walk-in; 8-12 person two-hour seating is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best for 8, not 12, with staggered browsing and coffee.
venues.capacity_notes: Small cafe bookshop; PAINTS should pair with Peckham Rye walk or nearby benches.
venues.why_fits_paints: It directly satisfies the raised Books & Coffee rule: bookshop plus coffee, not just coffee with vibes. The curation is local, readable and unpretentious. Its proximity to Peckham Rye makes it a useful weather backup too.
venues.anecdote_story: Southwark News interviewed owner Alastair Kenward about Rye Books' story since 2011 and its role as a local bookshop.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Hand-picked new books, fiction, children's books, culture/history titles and coffee.
venues.safety_notes: North Cross Road/East Dulwich daytime route is calm; use daylight for larger group.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 22 min walk or short bus - no qualifying bookshop+cafe within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Strict 10-min book backup not available; small seating capacity.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Tue-Sun 10:00-17:00, Sunday from 11:00; Instagram says open Tuesday-Sunday and Bar Lala late Fri/Sat for summer. Host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop/cafe appears walk-in; 8-12 person two-hour seating is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8 with advance heads-up; 12 may overwhelm the small room.
venues.capacity_notes: Small independent bookshop/cafe; living-room feel rather than large seating.
venues.why_fits_paints: Lala is the strongest Southwark Books & Coffee pick: real books, hot coffee, literary events and a room designed for lingering. It has enough press heat to be slightly obvious, but the offer is exactly PAINTS-shaped. The Camberwell location gives useful geographic balance.
venues.anecdote_story: Vogue reports Lala occupies the former Grove Lane Deli and has hosted authors including International Booker winner Banu Mushtaq.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary literary fiction, translated/small-press books, art/feminism/nature titles, coffee and cakes.
venues.safety_notes: Grove Lane/Camberwell is calm by day; route via Denmark Hill or bus corridors after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 22 min walk or short bus - no qualifying bookshop+cafe within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Strict 10-min backup unavailable; small-room group seating must be confirmed.

venues.venue_name: Review Bookshop + Bellenden Road coffee pairing
venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Tue-Sat 10:00-17:00 and Sun 11:00-17:00; Instagram says Tue-Sat 10:00-17:00 and Sun 10:00-16:00. Coffee partner hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-in accepted; 8-12 browsing/coffee seating depends on next-door or nearby cafe and is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works as book-browse plus nearby coffee, not as a two-hour seated in-shop event.
venues.capacity_notes: Tiny curated bookshop with strong events/literary identity; coffee must be paired nearby.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 10:00-12:00 before Bellenden Road lunch pressure.
venues.why_fits_paints: Review has real literary credibility and zine/magazine texture, but it does not clearly have on-site coffee. It only qualifies as a Tier B pairing if the host confirms a literal adjacent independent coffee setup. The shop itself is a beautiful Peckham anchor.
venues.anecdote_story: Time Out says Review was founded by Roz Simpson of Peckham Literary Festival and author Evie Wyld, and highlights its events programme.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Curated books, magazines, zines, events; coffee partner UNVERIFIED.
venues.safety_notes: Bellenden Road is calm and active by day; use Peckham Rye station routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 2 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Fails pure on-site coffee rule unless adjacent coffee partner is host-confirmed.

venues.opening_hours: Official site and Instagram list Thu-Sun 11:00-18:00, with occasional closures checked on Instagram/text.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-in bookshop; coffee is not confirmed on premises, so PAINTS coffee pairing is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good browse stop for a small group; not suitable as the sole seated Books & Coffee venue.
venues.capacity_notes: Two small rooms of secondhand books and zines; pair with nearby coffee.
venues.why_fits_paints: BOOKS has strong zine/secondhand character and a genuine off-grid Peckham feel. It is below Tier A because the coffee/seating requirement is not clearly met. As a backup to Review or a Peckham literary walk, it is highly useful.
venues.anecdote_story: The Times highlighted BOOKS as a two-room Peckham shop with cheap secondhand books, zines, small-press titles and cassettes.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Secondhand books, zines, small-press titles and cassettes; coffee pairing UNVERIFIED.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; Maxted/Oglander side streets are quiet, so regroup on Bellenden Road.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 2 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Does not satisfy on-site coffee requirement without a partner; occasional closure risk.

venues.opening_hours: Official contact page lists Tue 11:00-17:00, Wed 13:00-19:00, Fri 11:00-17:00, Sat 13:00-16:00 bookshop open too, with seasonal closure notes.
venues.walk_in_policy: Library/bookshop access appears walk-in during open hours; group visit and coffee provision are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Only suitable as a hosted archive/bookshop visit with prior confirmation; not a casual coffee sit.
venues.capacity_notes: Volunteer-run special library/archive; operationally fragile for PAINTS without pre-arrangement.
venues.why_fits_paints: The story and collection are exceptional, but it does not meet the coffee/seating rule cleanly. Include only as a Tier B cultural-book backup or special hosted session. It should not be sold as a normal bookshop cafe.
venues.anecdote_story: The Feminist Library was founded in 1975 to preserve women's liberation movement literature and is now in Southwark.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Feminist archive, books, pamphlets, periodicals and Saturday bookshop access.
venues.safety_notes: Use daytime and direct bus/rail routing; confirm building access.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 18 min walk or short bus - no qualifying 10-min bookshop cafe backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Coffee and group walk-in suitability unverified; volunteer opening hours can shift.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Closed; historic bookshop/cafe but not current.
Excellent drawing-focused institution, but live page says exhibition and library currently closed on the run date; not reliable for July 2026 sourcing.
Atmospheric but weaker live official current-hours/maker-curation evidence than Borough, Maltby, Blue and Peckham Salvage Yard.
Insufficient PAINTS evidence for independent natural-wine/gastro-pub story and group walk-in robustness compared with 40 Maltby Street and Diogenes.
Cemetery walks are not permitted by the PAINTS v6 safety list except official Highgate Cemetery tours; Nunhead must not be sourced.
Excellent park but primarily assigned to the Dulwich/Lambeth edge in prior sourcing; Southwark already has approved Southwark Park and Peckham Rye Park options, so this file avoids overreaching.
Interesting former transparent gallery space, but current programme evidence was too thin and appears archival rather than a reliable July 2026 venue.
Food-hall/venue feel is less producer-led and more templated than Borough, Maltby or Mercato; not enough PAINTS character for the market category.
Good independent cafe hours, but no substantive books/bookshop offer; fails the raised Books & Coffee rule.
Strong Rotherhithe story and useful walk anchor, but not enough paintings/sculpture/textiles/craft exhibition evidence for the Museum & Gallery Days category.
